codeReview.summary: Reviewed both diffs: original failing commit 342a5e1 and fix commit b4a2769. The prior failure was the broad heuristic in dashboardState.ts (`!sign_ready && onlinePeerCount >= threshold`) that misclassified policy-only blocking as nonce depletion. The fix replaces it with policy-aware gating (`peerPolicyAllowsRequestSign` + threshold check over policy-allowed online peers) and explicit runtime nonce-signal trust, then adds focused tests for policy-only, nonce-only, and mixed cases. This adequately resolves the reported false-positive Trigger Sync behavior while preserving genuine nonce-depletion handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-worker skill currently reads as requiring agent-browser manual verification, but this fix was implemented/reviewed as a pure state-derivation + unit-test change with no agent-browser invocation, while still marked as followedProcedure in handoff. The procedure wording appears overly strict/ambiguous for non-UI fix features.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the fix feature artifacts, transcript skeleton, prior failing review, and both relevant commit diffs (342a5e1 and b4a2769). The fix directly addresses the original blocking defect by preventing policy-only signing blocks from surfacing nonce depletion UI, and adds targeted tests that cover the previously failing scenario.
